$comment: IxoSynth Dashboard — Design DNA Layer (the contract every agent references). Tokens EXTRACTED from Kimi's template 'Connectome Constellation' (https://52pbcykolt4is.ok.kimi.link/) on 2026-06-28. This is the source of truth for color/type/effects; Kimi owns evolving it, all other agents consume it and never deviate without Kimi sign-off.

source.name: Connectome Constellation — Living Literature Map (Network Neuroscience)

stack.note: 2D renderer should match live/presentation/01-steering-wheel-dashboard.html (SVG + LENS); 3D is the new Three.js constellation.

schools.$comment: 9-tone muted community palette — one hue per group/lens. Map node.group -> a color.

semantic-hsl.$comment: shadcn tokens (space-separated HSL, used as hsl(var(--x))). Light theme as shipped.

show_styles.$comment: LEGO grammar — node.kind decides color (community), icon, AND show_style (the render template used when a node is OPENED). The graph stays one uniform model; only the open-view branches. Same pattern as shape/templates/.

magazine.feels: editorial spread, read like a story (cosmic-deck / gazette); media embedded inline (hero image, video, slideshow)

lego.feels: numbered build steps, exploded parts, snap A->B — instruction booklet

deepdive.feels: open-notebook critique / deep-dive surface; audio overview + sources

data.feels: business data — tables, charts, scorecards (e.g. Evito)

media.$comment: The CONTENT a library node points to is media-rich (blogs, articles, business data). Each library item carries a `cover` (hero thumb for the constellation node + card) and a `media[]` manifest. The node REFERENCES media by URL — it never embeds bytes. The show_style decides how the media renders when opened.
media.cover: single hero image/poster URL — what the graph node + library card show as a thumbnail

registry.$comment: Truth/content split. Library is polymorphic (many backends); registry is Postgres and only INDEXES them. Every node carries source={backend,ref,url} back to its home store. Content stays home; registry references it.
registry.note: BookStack uses MySQL/MariaDB (NOT Postgres) — index it via its REST API (/api/docs), never merge.

mapping.$comment: How the constellation template maps onto the IxoSynth Dashboard contract — it IS the same shape.
